Knowledge graphs represent relationships between entities. They are often used to enhance search systems and recommendation engines.
Multimodal AI models can process multiple types of data, such as text, images, and audio. These models are becoming increasingly important for real-world applications.

The transformer architecture revolutionized natural language processing. It introduced the attention mechanism, which allows models to focus on different parts of the input sequence when generating outputs. Transformers enable parallel computation and scale efficiently.

Large language models are deep learning models trained on massive text datasets. They can perform tasks such as question answering, summarization, translation, and code generation. Recent models use transformer architectures to understand and generate language.

An AI agent is a system that can perceive information, reason about it, and take actions using tools or APIs. Modern LLM agents often use planning strategies like ReAct or Planner-Executor to solve complex tasks.
Batch processing allows machine learning models to process multiple samples simultaneously. This improves computational efficiency and stabilizes training.
GPU acceleration significantly speeds up deep learning training. GPUs are well suited for the matrix operations required by neural networks.

LangGraph extends LangChain by enabling developers to build complex agent workflows. Instead of simple chains, LangGraph allows graph-based execution with loops, conditional logic, and state management.
ReAct is a reasoning framework where language models alternate between reasoning steps and actions. The model first explains its thought process, then calls tools such as search or calculation APIs.
Planner-Executor is a common architecture in agent systems. A planner generates a multi-step plan to solve a problem, while an executor performs each step using available tools.
LangChain is a framework for building applications powered by large language models. It provides tools for prompt management, document retrieval, memory handling, and tool integration.

Cosine similarity is a metric used to measure the similarity between two vectors. It calculates the cosine of the angle between them. Values closer to 1 indicate higher similarity, making it useful for semantic search systems.
Continuous integration and continuous deployment automate the software delivery pipeline. CI/CD tools help teams release software more frequently and reliably.

Attention mechanisms allow neural networks to weigh the importance of different input tokens. Instead of treating all inputs equally, attention dynamically assigns weights to tokens based on relevance, improving model performance in sequence tasks.

FAISS is an open-source library developed by Meta for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors. It supports billions of vectors and provides highly optimized algorithms for nearest neighbor search.
Monitoring and logging are essential for production systems. Tools such as Prometheus and Grafana help track system health and performance.
Feature engineering involves selecting or transforming variables to improve model performance. Good features can significantly enhance prediction accuracy.
OpenAI provides APIs for accessing large language models and embedding models. Developers can build chatbots, search systems, and automation tools using these APIs.
Sentence transformers are models designed to generate high-quality sentence embeddings. They are widely used for semantic search and clustering tasks.
Distributed systems allow applications to run across multiple machines. They improve scalability and fault tolerance for large workloads.
Vector similarity search finds the closest vectors to a query vector in a high-dimensional space. Efficient indexing algorithms are required when datasets contain millions of vectors.
Microservices architecture splits applications into smaller independent services. Each service can be developed, deployed, and scaled independently.
Message queues enable asynchronous communication between services. Popular systems include RabbitMQ, Kafka, and Redis streams.
Semantic search improves information retrieval by understanding the meaning of queries instead of matching keywords. It uses embeddings to find documents with similar semantic content.
PyTorch is a deep learning framework known for its flexibility and dynamic computation graph. Researchers often use PyTorch for experimentation and rapid model development.
Cross-validation is a technique used to evaluate machine learning models more reliably. The dataset is split into multiple subsets, and the model is trained and tested several times.
Data preprocessing is an important step in machine learning pipelines. It includes cleaning data, handling missing values, and normalizing features.
TensorFlow is a widely used machine learning platform developed by Google. It supports training and deploying large-scale neural networks across multiple devices.
Few-shot learning is a technique where a model learns a task from a small number of examples included in the prompt. This allows models to generalize to new tasks without additional training.
Evaluation metrics help measure model performance. Common metrics include accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score.
Python is one of the most popular programming languages for machine learning and AI development. Its rich ecosystem includes libraries such as NumPy, PyTorch, and TensorFlow.
Machine learning models often require large datasets for training. However, techniques such as transfer learning allow models to adapt to new tasks with smaller datasets.
Overfitting occurs when a machine learning model learns noise in the training data instead of general patterns. Techniques such as regularization and dropout help prevent overfitting.
Prompt engineering is the process of designing effective prompts to guide language models. Good prompts provide clear instructions, examples, and constraints to improve model output quality.

Embeddings are numerical vector representations of text, images, or other data. Machine learning models convert content into vectors so that semantic similarity can be measured using distance metrics such as cosine similarity or Euclidean distance.
Search engines index documents so they can be retrieved efficiently. Traditional systems rely on inverted indexes, while modern AI search uses vector similarity.
Load balancing distributes incoming requests across multiple servers. This improves system reliability and prevents overload on individual servers.

Retrieval-Augmented Generation, often called RAG, combines information retrieval with large language models. Instead of relying only on model training data, RAG systems retrieve relevant documents and provide them to the model as context before generating an answer.

Vector databases are specialized databases designed to store and search vector embeddings. They are commonly used in AI applications such as semantic search, recommendation systems, and retrieval augmented generation. FAISS, Milvus, and Pinecone are popular vector search systems.
API gateways act as entry points for backend services. They handle authentication, rate limiting, and routing of requests.
Caching improves application performance by storing frequently accessed data in memory. Redis and Memcached are common caching solutions.

Kubernetes is an orchestration system used to manage containerized applications at scale. It automates deployment, scaling, and management of containers. Kubernetes helps organizations run large distributed systems reliably across clusters of machines.
Natural language processing focuses on enabling computers to understand human language. Tasks include text classification, translation, and summarization.
Artificial intelligence continues to evolve rapidly. Advances in large language models and multimodal systems are transforming how humans interact with technology.
Recommendation systems analyze user behavior to suggest relevant content. They are widely used in e-commerce, streaming platforms, and social networks.

FastAPI is a modern Python web framework designed for building APIs quickly and efficiently. It is built on top of Starlette and Pydantic, providing automatic validation, serialization, and interactive API documentation. Developers often choose FastAPI because of its high performance, type safety, and ease of use when creating microservices or AI model serving APIs.

Docker is a containerization platform that allows developers to package applications and their dependencies into lightweight containers. Containers ensure that applications run consistently across different environments. Docker is commonly used in development, testing, and production environments to simplify deployment.
Speech recognition converts spoken language into text. Deep learning models have significantly improved the accuracy of modern speech systems.
Computer vision enables machines to interpret visual data such as images and videos. Applications include object detection and facial recognition.

Redis is an in-memory key-value data store widely used for caching, session storage, and real-time analytics. Because it keeps data in memory, Redis provides extremely low latency. It supports multiple data structures such as strings, lists, sets, and hashes, making it useful for many backend systems.
